acceptance_targets: [ch16_tv_room_interiority_drop] After revision, there is no stretch of 10 or more consecutive paragraphs between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:89 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:175 that lacks Carmen thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment, and at least one added interior beat in each cited block makes her exposure or alignment clearer than it is in the current draft.

[ch16_quiet_silence_scaffold_tic] In the revised chapter, no more than one of the cited beats still uses a generic quiet/silence scaffold, and the ending between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:307 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:309 lands on a concrete present consequence of Marcus's exit or Carmen's unfinished shift rather than on a generalized metaphor about things letting go.

findings.acceptance_test: After revision, there is no stretch of 10 or more consecutive paragraphs between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:89 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:175 that lacks Carmen thought, sensation, memory, or self-assessment, and at least one added interior beat in each cited block makes her exposure or alignment clearer than it is in the current draft.
findings.evidence: From snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:89 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:125, and again from snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:129 through snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:175, the confrontation runs in long blocks of dialogue and blocking before Carmen gets the next real internal beat at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:127 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:177.
findings.problem: The middle of the confrontation goes screenplay-thin. This is a close-third Carmen chapter, but the focalizer disappears for long runs while the chapter's central rupture happens, so the reader hears arguments without enough access to Carmen's bodily stress, legal exposure, professional reflexes, or shifting sympathies. The result is dramatically legible but less novelistically alive than the chapter needs to be.
findings.rewrite_direction: Thread Carmen-specific interior beats into the confrontation blocks between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:89 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:125, and between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:129 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:175. Use sensations, procedural thinking, self-correction, or quick moral calculations that can only belong to her and that change how the next line of dialogue lands, rather than saving her response for snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:127 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:177.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised chapter, no more than one of the cited beats still uses a generic quiet/silence scaffold, and the ending between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:307 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:309 lands on a concrete present consequence of Marcus's exit or Carmen's unfinished shift rather than on a generalized metaphor about things letting go.
findings.evidence: The chapter repeatedly falls back on the same hush scaffold at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:111, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:181, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:257, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:273, then caps the chapter with an abstract letting-go metaphor at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:309.
findings.problem: The prose keeps reaching for generic quiet/silence atmospherics instead of finding new pressure-specific language. Used once, these beats can tighten a room; used this many times in one chapter, they start to read as cinematic filler. The final leaves-as-letting-go image compounds the drift into generalized lyricism right after a chapter that otherwise works best when it stays concrete and institutional.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:111, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:181, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:223, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:257, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:273, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:309 to vary or cut the repeated quiet/silence scaffold. Replace most of those pauses with more scene-specific pressure: a concrete building noise, a bodily aftereffect, or a procedural consequence that matters to Carmen. Re-land the close between snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:307 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_16.md:309 on a concrete consequence rather than an abstract seasonal analogy.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This is a dark comedy about elderly agency, institutional failure, and moral compromise in a setting most fiction treats as a punchline or a tragedy but never both. The risks the novel must take: render the comedy of octogenarians as pharmaceutical distributors without condescending to them or turning them into lovable rogues; make Carmen's escalating complicity fully legible without moralizing about it; show the bodily realities of aging — incontinence, tremor, cognitive decline, falls, the smell of a body that doesn't leave a building — with unsentimental specificity; render the economic desperation of both healthcare workers and uninsured buyers without turning either into social-issue abstractions; give the moral ambiguity of the ring genuine weight by refusing to resolve it into either 'they were wrong' or 'they were heroes.' The novel must trust readers to sit with complexity. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. A nursing home that smells like potpourri is a nursing home written by someone who's never been in one.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism for bodily functions, aging, economic shame, institutional dysfunction, or the realities of nursing home care is a craft failure. The novel's comedy and humanity depend on rendering what polite fiction glosses over: what it smells like to clean someone who can't clean themselves, what it costs to count coins for a bus fare, what oxycodone sells for per milligram on the secondary market, what a concussion looks like on an 88-year-old skull, what it sounds like when someone with Parkinson's tries to hold a coffee cup steady, what Burt's dementia actually looks like up close. If the prose flinches from these specifics, the novel loses its authority.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the default for all dialogue. Uncontracted forms appear only for emphasis, character-specific formality (Agnes's low contraction level), Carmen under extreme stress reverting to formal speech, or deliberate register choices. In a contemporary New Jersey nursing home, stiff diction is the anomaly, not the norm.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate high-intensity scenes with reflective interiority. Vary scene count: single-scene chapters for maximum compression, three-scene chapters for range. Comic chapters (3, 9) should feel structurally different from quiet character studies (7, 8, 12, 15), which should feel different from crisis chapters (11, 16, 17). Follow a confrontation with a reflection, an escalation with a domestic scene, a comic set piece with an elegiac one. If two consecutive chapters share the same rhythm, one needs redesigning.
prose_style_profile.diction: Grounded and specific. Drug names (oxycodone, alprazolam, hydroxychloroquine), nursing procedures (med pass, pill count, charting, fall assessment), body realities (incontinence, tremor, laceration, scalp bleed). No purple prose, no literary performance. The style should feel like someone who works with their hands is telling you the truth about what she saw.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, varying by POV character. Carmen chapters are warm and sensory, anchored in the body and the immediate environment. Harry chapters are precise and calculating, with attention to numbers and angles. Agnes chapters are clinical and compressed. Lola chapters are winding and emotionally layered, with embedded memories. Earl chapters are spare and weighted, with silence doing as much work as language. Marcus chapters are kinetic, with physical movement and code-switching. Tammy chapters are flat and observational, her trained eye cataloguing what doesn't add up.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by chapter and POV. Carmen chapters favor medium sentences with occasional long runs when she's anxious. Harry chapters are short and clipped. Comic set pieces (chapters 3 and 9) move between fast inventory and slow domestic beats. Quiet chapters (7, 12, 15) slow down and let space onto the page. No chapter should feel rhythmically identical to the one before it.
